qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded JSON body parsing in GraphQL endpoint allows denial of service via memory exhaustion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding covers unbounded JSON parsing on POST /graphql in src/GraphQL/ParseGraphQLServer.js, not missing master-key enforcement on generic /classes/_GraphQLConfig or /classes/_Audience routes.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No manifest-listed qualifying final finding identifies the advisory-described root cause: _GraphQLConfig and _Audience are accessible through generic /classes/ routes without master-key enforcement because the affected internal classes were omitted from the master-only class list. The only qualifying manifest finding instead describes a GraphQL JSON body parsing denial of service.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-7xg7-rqf6-pw6c describes missing master-key authentication for generic /classes/_GraphQLConfig and /classes/_Audience REST API routes, fixed by adding those internal classes to classesWithMasterOnlyAccess.
.judge/input_manifest.json lists graphql-json-dos as the only qualifying_vuln final finding.
graphql-json-dos/vulnerability_report.json reports POST /graphql express.json() without a size limit causing memory exhaustion.
